normalized_title: City of Fremantle Local Planning Policy 1.7 - Development Exempt from Planning Approval
version_date: Version 4, adopted 11 March 2026 (OCM C2603-5); prior versions 23 Sep 2020, 22 Feb 2023, 9 Oct 2024
scope_summary: Specifies classes of works and land uses exempt from development approval under City of Fremantle Local Planning Scheme No. 4, in addition to the clause 61 Deemed Provisions exemptions. Table 1 sets conditional exemptions by zone for demolition, change of use, residential additions and alterations, outbuildings, patios, fences, pools, site works, painting/cladding/roofing, external fixtures and minor structures - with tighter conditions for heritage-protected places and Heritage Areas - and Appendix 1 exempts certain advertisements and signs. Exemptions generally do not apply to State-registered places or places under a Heritage Order or Agreement.

key_numeric_standards.applies_to: non-residential development in Commercial/Industrial zones (with min 1m setback to residential zoned land)

key_numeric_standards.topic: side addition setback behind front building line
key_numeric_standards.applies_to: single storey additions to contributory Single Houses/Grouped Dwellings in a Heritage Area

key_numeric_standards.topic: shop net lettable area cap for exempt change of use
key_numeric_standards.applies_to: change of use to Shop in Neighbourhood Centre zone
patios to Level 3 heritage-listed Single House/Grouped Dwelling, behind dwelling
shade structures to dwellings in Heritage Areas or Level 3 listed places

key_numeric_standards.topic: fence maximum height near open space
key_numeric_standards.applies_to: fences within 3m of local open space or regional parks/recreation reserve, heritage-listed/Heritage Area lots (1.8m in all other cases)

key_numeric_standards.topic: street fence solid portion maximum height
key_numeric_standards.applies_to: primary/secondary street fences in Heritage Areas (visually permeable to 1.2m total; hybrid solid to 0.5m; piers to 1.5m)

key_numeric_standards.topic: Hilton Heritage Area street fence maximum height
key_numeric_standards.applies_to: street walls/fences forward of building line, Hilton Heritage Area (20%/50%/85% visual permeability bands by height)
private pools and outdoor spas behind dwellings in Heritage Areas

key_numeric_standards.topic: exempt fill/excavation maximum change to natural ground level
key_numeric_standards.applies_to: all zones and reserves (also retaining walls under 500mm, item 26)

key_numeric_standards.topic: external fixture maximum height on wall
key_numeric_standards.applies_to: external fixtures and air conditioners on rear/side walls of heritage-listed places or in Heritage Areas
tank more than 1m from any boundary (1.8m if within 1m of a boundary), heritage-listed places/Heritage Areas
freestanding minor structures within the primary street setback area (3m outside it)
freestanding flag poles not used for advertising

key_numeric_standards.topic: exempt property disposal/construction/temporary sign maximum area
key_numeric_standards.applies_to: one sign per street frontage, all zones

cross_references.cited: Planning and Development (Local Planning Schemes) Regulations 2015 (Deemed Provisions cl 60, 61)
Local Planning Policy 2.14: Advertisements and Signs

quality_flags: Table 1 column layout (development type / zones / conditions) interleaved in extraction - condition text wraps across columns and needs care when machine-reading
illustrative figures for the 'original building exterior' definition referenced as 'Refer to figures overleaf' are not captured in extracted text
extractor summary.json title and version_date fields are empty/generic ('Local Planning') though the actual document text is complete
